column1: SAP-C02,"Domain 2: Design for New Solutions","Design for New Solutions","## SAP-C02 Domain 2: Design for New Solutions
This domain covers designing complex, highly scalable, enterprise-grade solutions using the full breadth of AWS services.

column3: S3 for large payloads, pass reference
SNS -> multiple Lambda, or SQS + Lambda

column1: Devices -> IoT Core (MQTT) -> Rules Engine -> Actions

column3: Local compute + ML on IoT devices
EFA (Elastic Fabric Adapter) - bypass OS kernel

column1: - Only works with Cluster Placement Group
- Use cases: Weather modeling, CFD, molecular dynamics
- **""Process millions of events""** -> Kinesis + Lambda (or EventBridge)
- **""GPU workloads in containers""** -> ECS/EKS on EC2 (not Fargate)
- **""Service-to-service communication""** -> App Mesh or Service Connect
- **""Ad-hoc queries on S3""** -> Athena (serverless, pay per scan)
- **""Real-time stream processing""** -> Kinesis Data Streams + Analytics
- **""Deliver streaming data to S3""** -> Kinesis Firehose
- **""Fine-grained data lake access""** -> Lake Formation (column/row level)
- **""HPC inter-node communication""** -> EFA + Cluster Placement Group
- **""High-throughput file system for HPC""** -> FSx for Lustre
- **Step Functions** for orchestration. Standard = long-running. Express = high-volume.
- **EventBridge** is the backbone of event-driven architecture (replaces CloudWatch Events).
- **SQS** decouples and buffers. SNS fans out. EventBridge routes intelligently.
- **Fargate** = serverless containers (simplest). EC2 launch type = more control, cheaper at scale.
- **EKS** when you need Kubernetes portability. ECS for AWS-native simplicity.
- **Athena** = serverless SQL on S3 (pay per TB scanned). Use Parquet + partitioning to reduce cost.
- **Redshift Spectrum** queries S3 data from Redshift (extend warehouse to data lake).
- **Lake Formation** provides fine-grained (column/row) access control on data lakes.
- **Kinesis Data Streams** = custom processing, multiple consumers. Firehose = delivery only.
- **Bedrock** = managed foundation models. SageMaker = build your own models.
- **EFA** = HPC networking (OS-bypass). Only works in Cluster Placement Groups.
- **FSx for Lustre** = HPC file system (sub-ms latency, integrates with S3).
- **Outposts** = AWS hardware on-premises. Local Zones = AWS in specific cities.
- **App Mesh** = Envoy-based service mesh for microservices traffic management."
